channel.title: Terminal on Nandaja
channel.description: Recent content in Terminal on Nandaja

item.title: Anti virus for Linux
item.description: Using an anti virus software in my Linux machine is something that I have not given much thought to. I know for the fact that the odds for a Linux system to get affected by a malware is pretty low. As of my understanding, the fact that Linux systems use open source software is a big bonus here. I mean, why would some one bother in sitting and writing a harmful program when everyone can see your code.

item.title: Quick tip - Installing a software in linux using files in .run format
item.description: The .run files are binary executable files. These files need to be executed to install the software. Such files are often considered as unsafe as we cannot find a way to uninstall them and we cannot say what the code will do to your system. But some games and apps are available that are packaged in this file format. Installing using .run file is rather easy. First of all download the required .

item.title: Recovering lost password in Ubuntu 12.04
item.description: Yesterday, While I was trying to help my friend with his lost Ubuntu password, I explored the following two methods to do this quite easily. First method is using the recovery mode. This method is pretty simple. In the grub menu, select the recovery mode. Then in the menu that appear, scroll down to the option ‘Drop to root shell prompt’. Now you will get a shell prompt with root authentication at the bottom of the screen.

item.title: perl warning Setting locale failed
item.description: Today when I was trying to build a package on a Debian VPS, It started reporting really annoying messages like these: perl: warning: Setting locale failed. perl: warning: Please check that your locale settings: LANGUAGE = (unset), LC_ALL = (unset), LANG = “en_US.UTF-8” are supported and installed on your system. perl: warning: Falling back to the standard locale (“C”). Then I was trying to install a new package, I got them again.
